name: MCP AWS Server
description: AWS's official MCP offering for AWS cloud services. Two forms: the managed AWS MCP Server (GA 2026-05-06), a fully managed remote exposing 15,000+ AWS API operations plus documentation search and a sandboxed run_script tool, accessed through the open-source MCP Proxy for AWS which bridges IAM SigV4 credentials to MCP's OAuth model; and the awslabs/mcp suite of open-source, service-scoped servers (DynamoDB, CDK, pricing, etc.). Powerful but requires strict IAM controls.

evidence.value: AWS APIs have 99.99% uptime SLA for most services
High success rates for API operations with proper retry logic
Supports multiple AWS services but integration complexity varies
Response times vary by service and region (100ms-2s typical)
Comprehensive error codes with automatic retry mechanisms
Managed server authenticates with IAM credentials via SigV4; supports read-only restriction through IAM policies or Service Control Policies and separation between human and agent permissions

iam_security.methodology: IAM security review; score raised from 78 as the GA managed server adds documented agent-specific IAM guidance, read-only policy patterns, and SCP-based governance

evidence.value: The open-source MCP Proxy for AWS bridges IAM (SigV4) to MCP's OAuth model, so short-lived IAM role credentials can be used instead of static access keys; self-hosted awslabs servers still commonly rely on locally configured credentials

credential_exposure_risk.methodology: Credential security analysis; modest raise from 60 since the managed server supports role-based short-lived credentials rather than requiring static keys

evidence.value: AI can create, modify, delete AWS resources including EC2, S3, databases
AI can provision expensive resources without built-in cost controls
Comprehensive audit logging via CloudTrail for all API actions
Managed server additionally publishes CloudWatch metrics under the AWS-MCP namespace and logs agent actions in CloudTrail for compliance
AI can read S3 buckets and database contents, sending data to LLM
AWS resource data and content sent to LLM provider for processing
No built-in PII detection when accessing S3, RDS, or other data stores
Can leverage VPC for network isolation but data still sent to LLM
AWS is compliant but sharing data with LLM provider affects compliance
Supports AWS KMS but data decrypted before sending to LLM
Comprehensive AWS SDK and service documentation
All AWS API actions logged in CloudTrail
First-party AWS implementation: open-source awslabs/mcp suite plus the managed AWS MCP Server (GA 2026-05-06) with published documentation site at awslabs.github.io/mcp

mcp_implementation.methodology: Implementation documentation review; raised from 72 as the implementation is now officially maintained by AWS with a dedicated docs site, replacing the previous community-maintained status

evidence.value: AWS now publishes MCP-specific security guidance: IAM/SCP read-only patterns, separation of human vs agent permissions, and per-server security documentation in awslabs/mcp

security_guidance.methodology: Security documentation review; raised from 75 due to official MCP-specific IAM governance guidance shipped with GA

evidence.value: Managed server requires installing the local MCP Proxy for AWS plus IAM configuration; awslabs servers install via uvx/pip; still requires AWS account and credential management
Managed server's call_aws tool executes any of 15,000+ AWS API operations across all services, plus documentation search/read and sandboxed server-side Python via run_script

api_coverage.methodology: API coverage assessment; raised from 85 as the GA managed server covers essentially the full AWS API surface

evidence.value: High reliability with 99.99% uptime for most services
Complex pricing model; AI can incur unexpected costs
Actively maintained first-party open-source suite with a large contributor base; managed server backed by AWS support channels

community_support.methodology: Community activity analysis; raised from 75 reflecting first-party AWS maintenance

code-generation.notes: Useful for generating infrastructure as code and automation scripts

customer-support.notes: Can manage support infrastructure but limited direct customer impact

content-creation.notes: Can manage S3-based content storage but not primary use case

data-analysis.notes: Excellent for analyzing data in S3, RDS, and other AWS data services

research-assistant.notes: Good for managing research data in AWS but data exposure concerns

legal-compliance.notes: Very high risk - can expose confidential data and modify critical infrastructure

healthcare.notes: Extreme risk for PHI exposure; HIPAA compliance nearly impossible with LLM sharing

financial-analysis.notes: High risk for sensitive financial data and infrastructure

education.notes: Useful for managing educational infrastructure and resources

creative-writing.notes: Can manage content storage but significant risk/benefit mismatch

best_for: DevOps engineers automating AWS infrastructure provisioning and management
Data analysts working with large datasets in S3 and RDS
strengths: Official first-party AWS offering: managed AWS MCP Server (GA 2026-05) plus the open-source awslabs/mcp suite
Managed server exposes 15,000+ AWS API operations through a small fixed tool set (call_aws, documentation search/read, sandboxed run_script)
IAM SigV4 authentication with support for read-only restriction via IAM policies or Service Control Policies
Excellent audit logging through CloudTrail plus CloudWatch metrics in the AWS-MCP namespace
Documented separation of human vs agent permissions for governance
No charge for the managed server itself; pay only for resources created
limitations: CRITICAL SECURITY RISK: with write-capable IAM permissions the AI can create, modify, delete infrastructure
Cost control risk - AI can provision expensive resources
Data in S3, RDS, and other services exposed to LLM provider
No built-in PII detection or sensitive data filtering
Managed server requires the local MCP Proxy for AWS since MCP clients speak OAuth 2.1, not SigV4
Managed endpoint regions limited (US East N. Virginia, Europe Frankfurt), though API calls can target any region
Potential for catastrophic infrastructure changes if IAM is misconfigured

metadata.license: Apache 2.0 (awslabs/mcp suite and MCP Proxy for AWS); managed server is a hosted AWS service
supported_platforms: All platforms with Python/uvx (awslabs servers); any MCP client via MCP Proxy for AWS (managed server)
metadata.remote_endpoint: https://aws-mcp.us-east-1.api.aws/mcp (via local MCP Proxy for AWS)
supported_services: 15,000+ AWS API operations via managed server (call_aws)
Service-scoped awslabs servers (DynamoDB, CDK, pricing, Step Functions, S3 Tables, etc.)
metadata.authentication: AWS IAM credentials (SigV4) bridged to OAuth via MCP Proxy for AWS; IAM roles or access keys for self-hosted servers
